description: PJAK (Partiya Jiyana Azad a Kurdistanê) is an Iranian Kurdish militant and political organisation founded in 2004, operating primarily from the Qandil Mountains along the Iran-Iraq border. Ideologically and organisationally linked to the Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK), PJAK follows the democratic confederalism philosophy of Abdullah Öcalan. The group has conducted armed operations against [Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) forces in the border regions of Kurdistan and Kermanshah provinces. Iran and Turkey have designated it a terrorist organisation. PJAK advocates for Kurdish self-governance within Iran through a decentralised, non-state model.
editorial_notes: PKK-linked Iranian Kurdish armed group. Based in Qandil Mountains. Designated terrorist by Iran, Turkey. Farzad Kamangar was accused of membership.
